transcript.text: SpaceX is planning to send 5 unmanned Starships to Mars in 2026, each test flight costs hundreds  of millions of dollars, but Elon Musk believes this is the future of humanity. Every failed  launch teaches SpaceX something new. They iterate faster than any traditional aerospace company,  learning from explosive failures to build better rockets. Starship is not just a rocket,  it's a fully reusable 2-stage system designed for Mars. The super heavy booster returns to Earth  while Starship continues to deep space. SpaceX is building a sustainable space economy.  From satellite internet to space tourism, the possibilities are endless. The question is not if,  but when.

video_description.response:
```
IDEO SUMMARY
Duration: 42.79s (based on frame timestamps)

The video opens with a field of green wheat. Text overlays the scene: "SpaceX" at the top left, "SpaceX's Mars Dream" at the bottom left, "SpaceX" in the middle, and "THE UPDATE DESK" at the bottom right.

The scene remains a wheat field as the text in the middle of the frame changes to "Starships to Mars in 2026. Each test" and then to "flight costs hundreds of millions of dollars,". The other text elements remain constant. The scene then transitions to a dark and stormy body of water with waves and dark clouds. The text changes to "The Cost of Innovation" at the bottom left, "Every" in the middle, and "THE UPDATE DESK" at the bottom right. The text in the middle then updates to "Every failed launch teaches SpaceX something".

Next, the screen goes completely black, with only "THE UPDATE DESK" visible at the bottom right. Finally, the scene shifts to a landscape view of mountains and trees at sunrise or sunset. The text changes to "The Super" and "Starship Technology" at the top left, "Starship is not just a rocket. It's" at the bottom center, and "THE UPDATE DESK" remains at the bottom right.

Note: This summary is based on direct observation of the first frame combined with detailed notes from subsequent frames.
```
